Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:14:16 UTC.EnglishDescribing Archives: A Content Standard >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Palmer Canfield papers
Canfield, Palmer
8771
.3 cubic feet.
1913-1931 Typescript report, letters, newspaper clippings, correspondence, memorandums, invitations, mayoral candidate promotional material, business, and seasonal cards, a booklet, and a scrapbook album of newspaper clippings of the New York politician and advocate of Prohibition, Palmer Canfield, document primarily Canfield's final Prohibition post (from 1929) as Federal Prohibition Director for Albany District, as well as his mayoral candidate campaign (1913-1921). 13250430
Content Description
The Palmer Canfield papers primarily document Canfield's final Prohibition post (from 1929) as "Dry Chief" for the "wet" leaning Albany District and material from his political and legal career, including his mayoral campaigns in Kingston (1913-1921), New York, dating from 1913 to 1931. The collection contains a typescript report and letters, newspaper clippings, correspondence, memorandums, invitations, mayoral candidate promotional material, business, and seasonal cards, a booklet, and a scrapbook album of newspaper clippings.
Biographical / Historical
Palmer Canfield was a New York politician and advocate of Prohibition, first as Special Assistant U.S. Attorney (1921) in the Southern District of New York and Legal Advisor at the Prohibition Department (1922) and later as Federal Prohibition Director for the State of New York (1923). Canfield had been an actively practicing attorney since 1904. He was also elected Mayor of Kingston, NY, from 1913 to 1921 on the "Honesty, Economy, Efficiency" platform. One of his main activities during his tenure as Mayor and before the National Prohibition, was the reduction of Kingston's salons to one-third of their original number (from 125 to 50). He was also one of the organizers of the Mayors Bureau of State, a Grand Chancellor of Knights of Pythia (1920-1921), and a member of the State and National Councils, Y.M.C.A., the executive committee N.Y. State Methodist Laymen's Association, the New York State Waterways Association, and more. He was married, and he had five children.
Preferred Citation
Palmer Canfield papers, #8771. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Bars (Drinking establishments) Drinking of alcoholic beverages -- United States. Liquor laws -- New York (State) New York (State) -- Politics and government. United States -- Politics and government. Prohibition. Prohibition -- Law and legislation Temperance Canfield, Palmer
13250430 Anonymous letter against Dry Force1929 February 13 <<<<<<< HEAD 11
Scope and Contents
Provocative letter, titled " A Valentine for the Dry Force and their Commissioner Old Mother Hubbard, who is such a dry old maid his bones rattle," possibly by a group of people, signed as the Repealers of Prohibition. The sender(s) encourage Canfield to read Times Union's letters to the editor about Prohibition Law repeal, including the actual newspaper clippings.Canfield Plans Survey for New Drive on Rum1929 January 06 12
Scope and Contents
Newspaper clipping, with subtitle "Farm relief first on Legislative Slate; Times Union, The Sunday Telegram. Albany NY".Canfield's Unpublished Letter to Kingston Daily Freeman1913 October 28 13
Scope and Contents
Flyer with an unpublished letter sent from Canfield as a candidate for Mayor to the Kingston Daily Freeman.Ephemera1913-193114
Scope and Contents
Nursery invoices; invitations; envelopes from Canfield's correspondence as the Federal Prohibition Director; election campaign cards for Palmer's candidacy as Mayor; Plamer's business and seasonal cards; booklet with the title "Manual for New York State Law Enforcement Officials and Local A.B.C. Boards. Program from the 15th Annual Reunion Dinner of the Ulster Country Society in NYC".Mayoral campaigns material1913 October 25 15
Scope and Contents
Speech of Palmer Canfield, Jr., from his mayoral campaign; election promotional flyers.Report on Federal Prohibition Activities 2nd Federal District, March 1-December 31, 19261927 January 19 16
Scope and Contents
Typescript report. Includes memorandums and correspondence (1927, January 14 -18)Resume and Correspondence1911, October; 1925, September; 1928, February.17
Scope and Contents
Several typescript copies of Canfield's resume. A letter from Natalie F. Cough, attorney, NYACK, NY in 1928. Canfield's correspondence as Federal Prohibition Director with G.E. Warner and Charles D. Hilles, the vice-chairman at the Republican National Committee.Scrapbook Album1929 January - June 11
Scope and Contents
Dozens of newspaper clippings, loose and affixed to scrapbook pages, detail Canfield's activity as the new Prohibition Administrator in the Albany district in 1929. ======= 11
